---
title: "Validation v6: Character-Boundary First Pass Fixation Position (FPFP)"
author: "Auto-generated validation report"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    code_folding: show
    df_print: paged
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
```

# 1. Introduction

This is the sixth validation report comparing **fixated** against
**analyzeeyedry** on `C1L1.asc` (a single-subject UMass EyeTrack 6 EyeLink
recording of Spanish reading data).

The primary goal of this report is to validate the **character-boundary-based
First Pass Fixation Position (FPFP)** computed by `get_landing_info()` using
the new `character_boundaries` argument (introduced alongside v5) against
the **First Pass Landing Position (`fplp`)** computed by analyzeeyedry.

## Comparison with v5

v5 used the **monospace / proportional** method:

> `fixated_char = floor(N × word_proportion) + 1`

where `word_proportion = (avg_x − x_start) / (x_end − x_start)` assumes
characters are equally wide.

v6 uses the **character-boundary** method:

> For each fixation, find the character from the `REGION CHAR` bounding boxes
> whose pixel interval `[x_start, x_end]` contains `avg_x`.

This is the same information that **analyzeeyedry** uses (it locates the
character by comparing the fixation's pixel x against the right-pixel boundary
of each character in order).  We therefore expect **near-perfect agreement**
between `fixated` v6 and analyzeeyedry `fplp`.

The analysis uses the same parameter set as v4 and v5 (`shorttime = 80`,
`longtime = 800`, `ttcutoff = 4000`, `truncate_at_display_on = TRUE`).

---

# 2. Setup

```{r packages}
pkg_root          <- file.path(getwd(), "..")
analyzeeyedry_dir <- file.path(getwd(), "analyzeeyedry")

if (!requireNamespace("analyzeeyedry", quietly = TRUE)) {
  install.packages(analyzeeyedry_dir, repos = NULL, type = "source")
}
# Always reinstall fixated from source: v6 requires character_boundaries in
# read_eyetrack_asc(), which may not be present in a previously installed build.
install.packages(pkg_root, repos = NULL, type = "source", quiet = TRUE)

library(analyzeeyedry)
library(fixated)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(knitr)

ASC_FILE  <- file.path(pkg_root, "data_to_incorporate", "C1L1.asc")
SHORTTIME <- 80L
LONGTIME  <- 800L
TTCUTOFF  <- 4000L

stopifnot(file.exists(ASC_FILE))
cat("ASC file:", ASC_FILE, "\n")
cat("fixated version:", as.character(packageVersion("fixated")), "\n")
cat("analyzeeyedry version:", as.character(packageVersion("analyzeeyedry")), "\n")
```

---

# 3. Parse C1L1.asc with `read_eyetrack_asc()`

```{r parse-eyetrack}
et <- read_eyetrack_asc(ASC_FILE, trial_pattern = "^[A-Za-z]")
cat(sprintf("Parsed %d trials\n", nrow(et$trial_db)))
cat(sprintf("Fixations: %d rows\n", nrow(et$fixations)))
cat(sprintf("Word boundaries: %d rows across %d trials\n",
            nrow(et$word_boundaries),
            n_distinct(et$word_boundaries$trial_nr)))
cat(sprintf("Character boundaries: %d rows across %d trials\n",
            nrow(et$character_boundaries),
            n_distinct(et$character_boundaries$trial_nr)))
```

Confirm character boundary coverage – every word should have at least one
character boundary entry:

```{r char-coverage}
cb_words <- et$character_boundaries |>
  dplyr::distinct(trial_nr, word_id)

wb_words <- et$word_boundaries |>
  dplyr::distinct(trial_nr, word_id)

coverage <- dplyr::left_join(wb_words, cb_words |> dplyr::mutate(has_cb = TRUE),
                              by = c("trial_nr", "word_id"))

cat(sprintf("Word boundaries with character data: %d / %d (%.1f%%)\n",
            sum(!is.na(coverage$has_cb)), nrow(coverage),
            100 * mean(!is.na(coverage$has_cb))))

# Preview first few character boundary rows
head(et$character_boundaries, 10)
```

---

# 4. analyzeeyedry reference analysis

```{r eyedry-analysis}
word_regions_edry <- asc_to_cnt(
  ASC_FILE, trial_pattern = "^[A-Za-z]",
  spillover_gap = 1L, spillover_size = 20L)
fix_edry   <- asc_to_fixations(ASC_FILE, subj = 1L, trial_pattern = "^[A-Za-z]")
fix_edry_r <- assign_regions_to_fixations(fix_edry, word_regions_edry)
nregmax_val <- max(word_regions_edry$nreg, na.rm = TRUE)

measures_edry <- compute_fixation_measures(
  fix_edry_r, word_regions_edry,
  shorttime = SHORTTIME, longtime = LONGTIME,
  ttcutoff  = TTCUTOFF,
  nregmax   = nregmax_val)
cat(sprintf("analyzeeyedry: measures for %d trials\n", nrow(measures_edry)))
```

---

# 5. Compute fixated FPFP

## 5.1 Pre-processing: truncate at display onset

```{r filter-fixations}
fix_raw <- et$fixations
tdb     <- et$trial_db

fix_filtered <- fix_raw |>
  dplyr::left_join(
    tdb[, c("trial_nr", "t_display_on", "t_display_off")],
    by = "trial_nr") |>
  dplyr::filter(end_time > t_display_on) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    straddle   = start_time <= t_display_on,
    start_time = dplyr::if_else(straddle, t_display_on, start_time),
    duration   = end_time - start_time
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(-t_display_on, -t_display_off, -straddle)

cat(sprintf("Fixations after display-on filter: %d (raw: %d)\n",
            nrow(fix_filtered), nrow(fix_raw)))
```

## 5.2 Landing info – monospace method (v5 baseline)

```{r landing-monospace}
fix_land_mono <- get_landing_info(fix_filtered, et$word_boundaries)
cat(sprintf("Monospace: fixations with word assignment: %d / %d\n",
            sum(!is.na(fix_land_mono$word_id)), nrow(fix_land_mono)))
```

## 5.3 Landing info – character-boundary method (v6, new)

```{r landing-charbounds}
fix_land_cb <- get_landing_info(
  fix_filtered,
  et$word_boundaries,
  character_boundaries = et$character_boundaries
)
cat(sprintf("Char-boundary: fixations with word assignment: %d / %d\n",
            sum(!is.na(fix_land_cb$word_id)), nrow(fix_land_cb)))

# How often does the character-boundary method give a different answer?
n_diff <- sum(
  !is.na(fix_land_mono$fixated_char) & !is.na(fix_land_cb$fixated_char) &
  fix_land_mono$fixated_char != fix_land_cb$fixated_char
)
n_both <- sum(!is.na(fix_land_mono$fixated_char) &
              !is.na(fix_land_cb$fixated_char))
cat(sprintf(
  "fixated_char differs between methods: %d / %d fixations (%.1f%%)\n",
  n_diff, n_both, 100 * n_diff / max(n_both, 1L)))
```

## 5.4 Extract FPFP for each trial × word

The helper below mirrors the FFD loop (same as v5) but is applied twice – once
for each landing method.

```{r compute-fpfp}
compute_fpfp <- function(fix_land, word_boundaries,
                          shorttime = 80L, longtime = 800L) {
  trials <- sort(unique(fix_land$trial_nr))
  result_rows <- vector("list", length(trials))
  ri <- 1L

  for (tr in trials) {
    tf <- fix_land[fix_land$trial_nr == tr, ]
    tf <- tf[order(tf$start_time), ]

    wb_tr <- word_boundaries[word_boundaries$trial_nr == tr, ]

    for (w in sort(unique(wb_tr$word_id))) {
      roi_row <- wb_tr[wb_tr$word_id == w, ]
      if (nrow(roi_row) == 0L) next

      x_s <- roi_row$x_start[[1L]]
      x_e <- roi_row$x_end[[1L]]

      px    <- tf$avg_x
      dur   <- tf$duration
      fc    <- tf$fixated_char
      n_fix <- nrow(tf)

      in_r   <- px >= x_s & px <= x_e
      past_r <- px >  x_e
      is_sht <- dur <= shorttime
      is_lng <- dur >  longtime

      fpfp_val <- NA_integer_
      for (i in seq_len(n_fix)) {
        if (past_r[i] && !is_sht[i]) break
        if (in_r[i]) {
          if (is_lng[i]) break
          if (is_sht[i]) next
          if (!is.na(fc[i])) {
            fpfp_val <- as.integer(fc[i]) - 1L   # convert to 0-indexed
          }
          break
        }
      }

      result_rows[[ri]] <- data.frame(
        trial_nr = tr,
        word_id  = as.integer(w),
        fpfp     = fpfp_val,
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
      ri <- ri + 1L
    }
  }
  dplyr::bind_rows(result_rows[seq_len(ri - 1L)])
}

fpfp_mono <- compute_fpfp(fix_land_mono, et$word_boundaries,
                           shorttime = SHORTTIME, longtime = LONGTIME) |>
  dplyr::rename(fpfp_mono = fpfp)

fpfp_cb <- compute_fpfp(fix_land_cb, et$word_boundaries,
                         shorttime = SHORTTIME, longtime = LONGTIME) |>
  dplyr::rename(fpfp_cb = fpfp)

cat(sprintf("Monospace FPFP: %d trial × word pairs, %d non-NA\n",
            nrow(fpfp_mono), sum(!is.na(fpfp_mono$fpfp_mono))))
cat(sprintf("Char-boundary FPFP: %d trial × word pairs, %d non-NA\n",
            nrow(fpfp_cb), sum(!is.na(fpfp_cb$fpfp_cb))))
```

---

# 6. Reshape analyzeeyedry output

```{r reshape-edry}
trial_key <- dplyr::tibble(
  trial_nr = seq_len(nrow(et$trial_db)),
  cond     = et$trial_db$cond,
  item     = et$trial_db$item)

pivot_edry_long <- function(wide_df, trial_key,
                             measures = c("ff","fp","tt","gp","oreg","ireg",
                                          "pfix","nfix","sp","fplp")) {
  meas_pat  <- paste0("^(", paste(measures, collapse="|"), ")_R\\d+$")
  meas_cols <- names(wide_df)[grepl(meas_pat, names(wide_df))]
  long <- wide_df |>
    tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = dplyr::all_of(meas_cols),
                        names_to  = c("measure","word_id"),
                        names_pattern = "^(.+)_R(\\d+)$",
                        values_to = "value") |>
    dplyr::mutate(word_id = as.integer(word_id)) |>
    tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = measure, values_from = value) |>
    dplyr::rename(ffd=ff, gd=fp, tvt=tt, gpt=gp) |>
    dplyr::left_join(trial_key, by = c("cond", "item"))
  long
}

edry_long <- pivot_edry_long(measures_edry, trial_key)
cat("analyzeeyedry long format:", nrow(edry_long), "rows\n")
```

---

# 7. Merge and compare

```{r merge}
compare_v6 <- edry_long |>
  dplyr::select(trial_nr, cond, item, word_id, fplp_edry = fplp) |>
  dplyr::inner_join(fpfp_mono, by = c("trial_nr", "word_id")) |>
  dplyr::inner_join(fpfp_cb,   by = c("trial_nr", "word_id")) |>
  dplyr::arrange(trial_nr, word_id)

cat(sprintf("Comparison table: %d rows\n", nrow(compare_v6)))
cat(sprintf("Rows with edry fplp non-NA: %d\n",   sum(!is.na(compare_v6$fplp_edry))))
cat(sprintf("Rows with mono fpfp non-NA: %d\n",   sum(!is.na(compare_v6$fpfp_mono))))
cat(sprintf("Rows with cb fpfp non-NA: %d\n",     sum(!is.na(compare_v6$fpfp_cb))))
cat(sprintf("Rows with all three non-NA: %d\n",
            sum(!is.na(compare_v6$fplp_edry) &
                !is.na(compare_v6$fpfp_mono) &
                !is.na(compare_v6$fpfp_cb))))
```

---

# 8. Agreement analysis

## 8.1 Summary statistics

```{r summary-stats}
full_summary <- function(df, col_edry, col_fix, label) {
  d    <- df[[col_fix]] - df[[col_edry]]
  ref  <- pmax(abs(df[[col_edry]]), 1)
  re   <- abs(d) / ref
  both <- !is.na(df[[col_edry]]) & !is.na(df[[col_fix]])
  n    <- sum(both)
  r_val <- if (n >= 3L) {
    round(cor(df[[col_edry]][both], df[[col_fix]][both],
              use = "complete.obs"), 3)
  } else NA_real_
  data.frame(
    measure         = label,
    n               = n,
    pearson_r       = r_val,
    pct_exact       = round(100 * mean(abs(d[both]) < 1,   na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    pct_1char       = round(100 * mean(abs(d[both]) <= 1,  na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    pct_2char       = round(100 * mean(abs(d[both]) <= 2,  na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    mean_abs_diff   = round(mean(abs(d[both]), na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    median_abs_diff = round(median(abs(d[both]), na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}

summary_tbl <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  full_summary(compare_v6, "fplp_edry", "fpfp_mono",
               "Monospace (v5 baseline)"),
  full_summary(compare_v6, "fplp_edry", "fpfp_cb",
               "Char-boundary (v6, new)")
)

knitr::kable(
  summary_tbl,
  col.names = c("Method", "N", "r",
                "% exact", "% ≤1 char", "% ≤2 chars",
                "Mean |diff| (chars)", "Median |diff| (chars)"),
  caption = paste0("v6: agreement between fixated FPFP methods ",
                   "and analyzeeyedry fplp.  Both measures 0-indexed.")
)
```

The character-boundary method (v6) should show substantially better agreement
(higher *r*, higher % exact, lower mean absolute difference) compared to the
monospace method (v5 baseline) because it uses the same `REGION CHAR` pixel
data as analyzeeyedry.

## 8.2 Scatter plots

```{r scatter-plots, fig.width=10, fig.height=5}
plot_mono <- compare_v6 |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(fplp_edry), !is.na(fpfp_mono)) |>
  dplyr::mutate(method = "Monospace (v5)", fpfp = fpfp_mono)

plot_cb <- compare_v6 |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(fplp_edry), !is.na(fpfp_cb)) |>
  dplyr::mutate(method = "Char-boundary (v6)", fpfp = fpfp_cb)

plot_df <- dplyr::bind_rows(plot_mono, plot_cb)

ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = fplp_edry, y = fpfp)) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.35, size = 0.9, colour = "#2166ac",
              width = 0.2, height = 0.2) +
  facet_wrap(~method) +
  labs(title = "FPFP: analyzeeyedry fplp vs fixated (both methods)",
       subtitle = "Both measures 0-indexed; dashed = perfect agreement",
       x = "analyzeeyedry fplp (0-indexed)",
       y = "fixated FPFP (0-indexed)") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11)
```

## 8.3 Distribution of absolute differences

```{r diff-distribution, fig.width=10, fig.height=4}
diff_df <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  compare_v6 |>
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(fplp_edry), !is.na(fpfp_mono)) |>
    dplyr::mutate(abs_diff = abs(fpfp_mono - fplp_edry),
                  method   = "Monospace (v5)"),
  compare_v6 |>
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(fplp_edry), !is.na(fpfp_cb)) |>
    dplyr::mutate(abs_diff = abs(fpfp_cb - fplp_edry),
                  method   = "Char-boundary (v6)")
)

ggplot(diff_df, aes(x = abs_diff, fill = method)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1, colour = "white", alpha = 0.8,
                 position = "identity") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", colour = "red",
             linewidth = 0.6) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:max(diff_df$abs_diff, na.rm = TRUE)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Monospace (v5)"    = "#2166ac",
                               "Char-boundary (v6)" = "#d6604d")) +
  facet_wrap(~method) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of |fixated FPFP − analyzeeyedry fplp|",
       subtitle = "Red dashed line = exact match threshold (0.5 chars)",
       x = "Absolute difference (characters)", y = "Count",
       fill = "Method") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```

## 8.4 Cross-tabulation – character-boundary method

```{r crosstab-cb}
both_known_cb <- compare_v6 |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(fplp_edry), !is.na(fpfp_cb))

ct_cb <- table(edry    = both_known_cb$fplp_edry,
               fixated = both_known_cb$fpfp_cb,
               useNA   = "ifany")
cat("Cross-tabulation of fplp (analyzeeyedry) vs fpfp_cb (char-boundary):\n")
print(ct_cb)

n_both_cb  <- nrow(both_known_cb)
n_match_cb <- sum(both_known_cb$fplp_edry == both_known_cb$fpfp_cb, na.rm = TRUE)
cat(sprintf("\nExact match: %d / %d (%.1f%%)\n",
            n_match_cb, n_both_cb, 100 * n_match_cb / max(n_both_cb, 1L)))
```

## 8.5 Cross-tabulation – monospace method (v5 baseline)

```{r crosstab-mono}
both_known_mono <- compare_v6 |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(fplp_edry), !is.na(fpfp_mono))

ct_mono <- table(edry    = both_known_mono$fplp_edry,
                 fixated = both_known_mono$fpfp_mono,
                 useNA   = "ifany")
cat("Cross-tabulation of fplp (analyzeeyedry) vs fpfp_mono (monospace):\n")
print(ct_mono)

n_both_mono  <- nrow(both_known_mono)
n_match_mono <- sum(both_known_mono$fplp_edry == both_known_mono$fpfp_mono,
                    na.rm = TRUE)
cat(sprintf("\nExact match: %d / %d (%.1f%%)\n",
            n_match_mono, n_both_mono,
            100 * n_match_mono / max(n_both_mono, 1L)))
```

## 8.6 Cases where the character-boundary method still disagrees with analyzeeyedry

```{r disagreements-cb}
disagree_cb <- compare_v6 |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(fplp_edry), !is.na(fpfp_cb),
                fplp_edry != fpfp_cb) |>
  dplyr::mutate(diff_cb = fpfp_cb - fplp_edry) |>
  dplyr::select(trial_nr, cond, item, word_id,
                fplp_edry, fpfp_cb, diff_cb) |>
  dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(abs(diff_cb)))

cat(sprintf("%d rows where char-boundary FPFP still differs from analyzeeyedry:\n",
            nrow(disagree_cb)))
if (nrow(disagree_cb) > 0L) {
  print(head(disagree_cb, 30))
}
```

## 8.7 How much did the character-boundary method improve over monospace?

```{r improvement}
compare_v6 <- compare_v6 |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    abs_diff_mono = abs(fpfp_mono - fplp_edry),
    abs_diff_cb   = abs(fpfp_cb   - fplp_edry),
    improved      = dplyr::case_when(
      is.na(abs_diff_mono) | is.na(abs_diff_cb) ~ NA_character_,
      abs_diff_cb <  abs_diff_mono               ~ "Improved",
      abs_diff_cb == abs_diff_mono               ~ "Same",
      abs_diff_cb >  abs_diff_mono               ~ "Worse"
    )
  )

compare_v6 |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(improved)) |>
  dplyr::count(improved) |>
  dplyr::mutate(pct = round(100 * n / sum(n), 1)) |>
  knitr::kable(
    col.names = c("Change vs monospace", "N", "%"),
    caption   = paste0("How often did the char-boundary method improve, ",
                       "maintain, or worsen agreement with analyzeeyedry?")
  )
```

---

# 9. NA comparison

```{r na-comparison}
compare_v6 |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    edry_na = is.na(fplp_edry),
    mono_na = is.na(fpfp_mono),
    cb_na   = is.na(fpfp_cb)
  ) |>
  dplyr::count(edry_na, mono_na, cb_na) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    status = dplyr::case_when(
      !edry_na & !mono_na & !cb_na ~ "All three non-NA",
      !edry_na & !mono_na &  cb_na ~ "edry + mono non-NA, cb NA",
      !edry_na &  mono_na & !cb_na ~ "edry + cb non-NA, mono NA",
      !edry_na &  mono_na &  cb_na ~ "Only edry non-NA",
      edry_na  & !mono_na & !cb_na ~ "Only fixated non-NA (both)",
      edry_na  & !mono_na &  cb_na ~ "Only mono non-NA",
      edry_na  &  mono_na & !cb_na ~ "Only cb non-NA",
      TRUE                         ~ "All NA"
    )
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(status, n) |>
  knitr::kable(caption = "NA pattern across analyzeeyedry fplp, monospace FPFP, and char-boundary FPFP")
```

---

# 10. Discussion

## Character-boundary method vs monospace

The **character-boundary** method implemented in v6 uses the same
`REGION CHAR` pixel bounding boxes that analyzeeyedry reads from the ASC file.
Because both systems consult the same source of truth for character positions,
the expected agreement rate is substantially higher than the monospace method.

Residual disagreements (if any) most likely arise from:

1. **Word-boundary edge cases**: `fixated` and analyzeeyedry may assign
   a fixation very near a word boundary to different words if their spatial
   matching rules differ slightly.  Once a fixation is assigned to a different
   word, the FPFP for both that word and its neighbour will disagree.

2. **Region spillover**: analyzeeyedry applies a *spillover* correction
   (gap and size parameters) when building word regions from character data.
   `fixated` merges character bounding boxes directly.  For most words the
   regions agree, but for words adjacent to gaps the pixel ranges may differ.

3. **Long fixation handling**: analyzeeyedry discards FPFP when a long
   fixation (`duration > longtime`) lands on the word during the first pass.
   `fixated` implements the same rule; any remaining discrepancy in `NA` values
   would indicate a difference in how straddling fixations or the `t_display_on`
   filter is applied.

## Conclusion

The character-boundary method is strongly recommended over the monospace
method when `REGION CHAR` data are available in the ASC file.  The monospace
method remains a useful fallback when character-level bounding boxes are not
recorded.

---

```{r session-info}
sessionInfo()
```
